stimulus: Sculptures by twentieth-century British ______blank were paired in Forms in Conversation, an exhibition at the Yorkshire Sculpture Park that examined the friendships and artistic exchanges shaping postwar modernism in Britain.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The role term “twentieth-century British sculptors” is indefinite—many sculptors fit that description—so the coordinated proper noun “Henry Moore and Barbara Hepworth” functions as a restrictive appositive that supplies essential identification. Restrictive appositives take no punctuation around them.

Choice B is incorrect because it brackets the names with commas, falsely treating the appositive as nonrestrictive. Choice C is incorrect because a colon after “sculptors” treats the names as a list following an independent introduction, and the comma between two coordinated names is also unwarranted. Choice D is incorrect because the commas separate the subject from its essential identifier and incorrectly insert a comma between two items joined by “and.”
